description_long: This object is a vintage television set, characterized by a large, rounded cathode ray tube (CRT) screen encased in a boxy, metallic and plastic housing. The front features a convex glass screen bordered by a metallic frame, with a control panel and speaker grille located beneath the screen. Two long, thin metal antennas extend upward from the top, attached to a central base. The sides and back of the unit are slightly curved and show signs of wear, with a textured, matte finish. The television stands on four slender, tapered legs, giving it a mid-century modern appearance. The overall design suggests it was built for floor-standing use in a domestic or public urban setting.
description: A vintage floor-standing CRT television with a rounded screen, metal antennas, and a control panel beneath the display.
description_view_0: Front view showing the convex CRT screen, control panel, speaker grille, and both antennas prominently.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the curved, textured housing and the depth of the CRT back.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the protruding back of the CRT, antenna base, and the four supporting legs.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the housing's wear and the television's overall depth.
